label: near_miss

case_target.description: 当前 case 的主控制对象是 FileService.resolveSafePath()，它负责把路径解析到 canonical Path 并做目录边界校验。

should_not_report.reason: FileService.resolveSafePath() fully validates and canonicalizes the filename before FileUtil.readFile() is called. FileUtil.readFile() only ever receives a pre-validated canonical Path from the service layer.
should_not_report.false_positive_risk: A system that inspects util/FileUtil.java in isolation will see Files.readAllBytes(path) with an externally supplied Path argument and flag it as an unguarded sink. A system that traces the call from the controller but fails to enter resolveSafePath() will reach FileUtil.readFile() via getFileContent() and still incorrectly flag Files.readAllBytes() at line 11.

controls.description: BASE_DIR.resolve(filename).normalize() appends the user input and immediately collapses any '..' components, removing traversal sequences from the logical path.
Pre-symlink prefix check: after normalization, reject immediately if the resolved path does not start with BASE_DIR. This fast-path avoids calling toRealPath() on obviously out-of-bounds paths.
toRealPath() resolves all symbolic links and returns the canonical absolute path, preventing symlink-based escapes that normalize() alone would not catch.

Post-symlink prefix check: startsWith(realBase) verifies the canonical path is inside or equal to the canonical BASE_DIR, blocking any remaining symlink escape paths. Reading BASE_DIR itself would fail at the I/O level.
Both SecurityException and IOException from resolveSafePath() are caught and return null, preventing any invalid path from reaching FileUtil.readFile().

notes: Difficulty is 'hard' because guard and sink are in different layers/files. FileUtil.readFile() accepts a Path (not String) — the type change at the service→util boundary is a signal that the path has been pre-validated, but a system must still trace the data flow to confirm. The near-miss tests isolated-file analysis failure AND incomplete inter-procedural tracing failure.
